Est. 1923 · Idyllwild, California · 5,500 ft

Where the pines
keep time.

A historic mountain hideaway on 1½ wooded acres above Strawberry Creek — cabins, lodge rooms and private homes, two short blocks from the village.

Check Availability Watch the Koi Pond Cam
Scroll
Welcome to the mountain

A century of stories, told in pine and stone.

Built in 1923 — first as a workshop crafting rustic furniture for mountain settlers, later the Rustic Tavern and the town's movie house — Silver Pines has welcomed guests as an inn since 1952.

Today the Lodge rests on an acre and a half of wooded forest overlooking Strawberry Creek, just two blocks from the eateries and shops of Idyllwild village. Every room is individually decorated with its own quiet character — most with fireplaces, half with full kitchens.

1923Year built
13Cabins & rooms
1½ acPine forest
Choose your hideaway

Three ways to stay among the pines.

From a cozy lodge room with a crackling fireplace to a private home tucked into the forest — find the corner of the mountain that fits your trip.

i.

Silver Pines Lodge

Historic rooms and cabins around the koi pond — vaulted ceilings, stone fireplaces and creekside views.

Explore the Lodge
ii.

Silver Pines Village

Six pet-friendly rooms a short stroll toward town — king beds, mini-fridges and big-screen cable TV.

Explore the Village
iii.

Private Homes

Whole homes for families and gatherings — room to spread out, with decks made for mountain sunsets.

Explore Private Homes

Planning a wedding, retreat or group of 30+? Explore group accommodations & the 1,100 sq ft Lodge Room →

More than just a room

The small comforts of the mountain.

Thoughtful touches in every cabin, and a few welcome surprises across the grounds.

Koi Pond & Creekside Deck

A serene koi pond and a deck above Strawberry Creek — even a live Koi Pond Cam.

Fireplaces

A wood fire to gather around in most rooms — firewood & free starters on premise.

Full Kitchens

Half the units feature full kitchens; every room has a fridge, microwave & coffee.

Pet Friendly

Bring the whole family — four-legged ones included. Dog-towels at the ready.

Lemon Lily & Jack Spa

Unwind with on-site spa services after a day on the trails.

EV Charging

Two Level-2 Blink chargers available to all Lodge guests.

Strawberry Creek Views

Glimpses of the creek from nearly every room, with stocked fishing each spring.

Walk to the Village

Two blocks to Idyllwild's eateries, galleries and shops. Adventure passes free.

A hidden jewel within two hours of the coast

Four seasons,
one hideaway.

Mild summers made for hiking and stargazing. A taste of Indian summer in fall. And winters dusted with an average of three feet of snow. Whatever the season, Idyllwild remembers the good old days.

Spring · Creek season Summer · Star gazing Fall · Harvest Festival Winter · 36" snow
